title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S20
body:
**Scene 20: 25:30–26:30 · The Photograph Lifted**

HERO FRAME: Close on Walter's hand picking up the framed photograph of his mother from the dresser · the silver frame catching the warm light · the dried wildflowers in the vase beside it · the photograph's smile seems brighter

MOTION: kling — Walter's hand lifts the frame, holds it against his chest (we see the back of the frame and his arm). The dried wildflowers seem to lift slightly, almost as if rejoicing. The dresser drawer beneath the photograph slides shut by itself.

MOOD: The small thing carried into the great thing.

AUDIO: Walter (quietly): *"May I bring something with me?"*
Visitor: *"Bring what you love."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
